sections.title: High Priority
sections.content: Seizures require immediate protection and monitoring. Status epilepticus (>5 minutes) is a medical emergency.

sections.title: Generalized Tonic-Clonic
sections.content: Full body convulsions, loss of consciousness.

steps.title: Ensure Safety
steps.subtitle:
Stay calm, clear area of hazards and protect patient from injury.

Ease patient to the floor if needed. Clear nearby objects.

Place something soft under the head. Loosen tight clothing around the neck.

  • Do NOT hold them down.
  • Do NOT put anything in their mouth.
  • Do NOT give food or water during seizure.
  • Do NOT perform rescue breaths during active convulsions.

steps.title: Time the Seizure
steps.subtitle: Note start time and duration of seizure activity. Most seizures last 1–3 minutes.

steps.title: Position Patient
steps.subtitle: Turn patient on side if possible to maintain airway

steps.title: Call Emergency Services & Transport Decision
steps.subtitle:
Call 911 if:
• Seizure lasts longer than 5 minutes or patient is injured.
• Repeated seizures without regaining consciousness.
• First known seizure.
• Person is pregnant.
• Person is diabetic.
• Person is injured.
• Seizure occurred in water.
• Breathing problems occur after seizure.

steps.title: Post-Seizure Care
steps.subtitle: After Seizure Stops (Post-ictal Phase), Stay with patient, they may be confused, sleepy, or embarrassed, reassure and orient them.

steps.title: Monitor Recovery
steps.subtitle: Assess for injuries and monitor breathing
